title: ECB research agenda
summary: This page is a structural overview of the ECB's research agenda, describing research areas and organizational coordination but containing no actual data releases, policy decisions, or market-relevant findings. The page lists seven research topic areas: monetary policy, fiscal policy, international macro, labour markets, forecasting, macro-finance, and financial institutions.
key_points: The ECB's research is coordinated by the Directorate General Research under the Research Coordination Committee

Research areas include: monetary policy/strategy, fiscal policy within EMU, international macro and finance, labour/product markets, forecasting and business cycle analysis, macro-finance and systemic risk, and financial institutions/microprudential policy
The page contains no publication date
No specific research findings, economic data, or policy recommendations are presented
The page functions as a navigation hub with 'Read more' links to individual topic pages
why_it_matters: This source does not contain actionable market information. It is an organizational landing page describing the ECB's research priorities. No investment-relevant data, policy signals, or economic forecasts are presented.
